statement.markdown: Write a function to validate a Git commit hash. A valid commit hash is 40 characters long and consists of hexadecimal digits (0-9, a-f, A-F). Your function should return `true` if the commit hash is valid and `false` otherwise.
statement.inputFormat: The input is a string representing the Git commit hash.
statement.outputFormat: The output is a boolean indicating whether the commit hash is valid.
statement.notes: You should not consider empty strings as valid commit hashes.

hints.text: Use a regular expression to match the commit hash pattern

editorial.title: Fixing Git Commit Hash Validation
editorial.intuition: Regular expressions are useful for pattern matching
editorial.bruteForce: You can try to implement a brute-force approach to check each character of the commit hash
editorial.betterSolution: Use a regular expression to match the commit hash pattern
editorial.optimalSolution: Use the `RegExp.test()` method to match the commit hash pattern
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The regular expression `^[0-9a-fA-F]{40}$` matches the commit hash pattern
